back_translation: We who are strong ought to bear the weaknesses of those who are weak — and not to please ourselves
Let each of us please his neighbor in what is good, for the purpose of edification
For even Christ did not (royally) please Himself — but as it is written: 'The insults of those who insult You have fallen on Me'
For all things that were written before, were written to teach us — so that through endurance and the encouragement of the Scriptures, we might have hope
May the God of endurance and encouragement (royally) grant you to think the same toward one another, according to the model of Christ Jesus
So that you may praise the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ — with one heart and with one mouth
Therefore, welcome one another, just as Christ (royally) welcomed you — for the (royal) glory of God
For I say that Christ (royally) became a servant of those who are circumcised, for the truth of God — to confirm the (royal) promises given to the patriarchs
And so that the Gentiles may give (royal) glory to God for His (royal) mercy. As it is written: 'Therefore I will praise You among the Gentiles, and I will sing praises to Your (royal) name'
And again it says: 'Gentiles, rejoice together with His (royal) people'
And again: 'Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles — and let all peoples extol Him'
And again, Isaiah says: 'The Root of Jesse will come — and the One who (royally) arises to rule the Gentiles — the Gentiles will hope in Him'
May the God of hope (royally) fill you with all joy and peace in believing — so that you may overflow with hope by the power of the Holy (royal) Spirit
And I myself am convinced concerning you, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness — completely filled with all knowledge — able to admonish one another
But I have written to you boldly in some matters — to remind you — because of the grace given to me by God

So that I may be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles — serving as a priest with the good news of God — so that the offering, which is the Gentiles, may be acceptable — (royally) sanctified by the Holy (royal) Spirit
Therefore, I have joyful rejoicing in Christ Jesus — in the matters concerning God
For I will not dare speak of anything except what Christ (royally) accomplished through me — to lead the Gentiles to obedience — by word and deed
By the power of signs and wonders, by the power of the (royal) Spirit — so that I have fully proclaimed the good news of Christ from Jerusalem all around to Illyricum
I have aspired to proclaim the good news where Christ has not yet been (royally) known — so that I would not build on someone else's foundation
But as it is written: 'Those who have never been told about Him will see — and those who have never heard will understand'
For this reason I have been hindered many times from coming to you
But now there is no longer place for me in these regions — and I have longed for many years to come to you
When I journey to Spain — for I hope to stop and see you on the way, and that you would send me on from there — after I have first been satisfied with you for a while
But now, I am traveling to Jerusalem to serve the saints
For Macedonia and Achaia were pleased to make a contribution-fellowship for the poor among the saints in Jerusalem
They were pleased — and indeed they are debtors to the Jews — for if the Gentiles have shared in the Jews' spiritual things, they are debtors to minister to them in the material things as well
Therefore, when I have finished this and sealed this fruit for them — I will go on to Spain by way of you
And I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ
Brothers, I urge you — by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the (royal) Spirit — to join me in struggle in prayer to God on my behalf
So that I may be delivered from those who are disobedient in Judea — and so that my ministry to Jerusalem may become acceptable to the saints
So that, by the (royal) will of God, I may come to you with joy and be refreshed together with you
May the God of peace be with all of you. Amen
